name: Claims Processing System
description: Core claims processing system handling end-to-end claim lifecycle from FNOL through settlement. Processes approximately 45,000 claims per month across all lines of business.
nfr_encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

name: Claims Portal
description: Customer and adjuster-facing web portal for claims submission, tracking, and management. Supports both desktop and mobile access.

name: Claims Data Platform
description: Analytics and reporting platform for claims data, providing real-time dashboards and batch reporting capabilities.

name: Legacy Claims API
description: Legacy SOAP-based claims API. Scheduled for decommission by Q3 2026. Still serves 15% of integration traffic from partner systems.

name: Adjudication API
description: REST API service handling claim adjudication decisions. Orchestrates rules engine evaluation, fraud checks, and settlement calculations.

name: Rules Engine
description: Business rules engine for claims adjudication. Contains 2,400+ rules across auto, property, and liability lines.

name: Claims Database
description: Primary claims data store. Aurora PostgreSQL with read replicas for reporting queries.

name: Event Router
description: Kafka-based event routing for claims domain events. Handles FNOL, status change, payment, and notification events.

name: Claims Notification Service
description: Handles email, SMS, and push notifications for claims status updates, document requests, and settlement communications.

name: Claims Reporting Service
description: Generates operational and regulatory reports. Includes daily loss runs, monthly reserve reports, and ad-hoc analytics.

name: Document Storage Service
description: Manages claim-related documents including photos, PDFs, police reports, and medical records. Integrates with OCR for auto-extraction.

name: Claims Web UI
description: React-based single-page application for claims management. Supports both customer self-service and adjuster workflows.

name: Claims Mobile BFF
description: Backend-for-Frontend service optimized for mobile clients. Aggregates data from multiple backend services.

name: Claim Adjudication
description: The ability to evaluate a claim against policy terms, coverage rules, and fraud indicators to produce an adjudication decision.

name: First Notice of Loss
description: The ability to receive and register initial loss notifications across all channels (web, mobile, phone, email).

name: Claim Settlement
description: The ability to calculate, authorize, and execute claim payments including partial payments, reserves, and recoveries.

name: Subrogation
description: The ability to identify, pursue, and recover subrogation claims from third parties.

name: Fraud Detection
description: The ability to detect, flag, and investigate potentially fraudulent claims using rules-based and ML-based indicators.

name: FNOL Process
description: End-to-end First Notice of Loss process from initial notification through claim creation and assignment.

name: Adjudication Process
description: Claim evaluation process including coverage verification, damage assessment, liability determination, and settlement calculation.

name: Settlement Process
description: Payment authorization and execution process for approved claims including partial payments, supplements, and final settlements.

name: Subrogation Recovery Process
description: End-to-end subrogation workflow: identify recovery opportunity → send demand letter → negotiate with third-party insurer → collect payment → close recovery. Average cycle time: 45 days. Recovery rate: 62% of identified opportunities.

name: Fraud Investigation Process
description: SIU investigation workflow triggered by fraud scoring above threshold (>0.7). Steps: case assignment → evidence collection → interview scheduling → determination → referral/closure. SLA: initial review within 48 hours, determination within 30 days.

name: Catastrophe Claims Process
description: Specialized high-volume claims process activated during declared catastrophe events (hurricanes, hail, wildfire). Includes: CAT declaration trigger → surge staffing → field adjuster deployment → expedited FNOL intake → batch adjudication → accelerated settlement. Handles 10x normal volume with modified SLAs.

name: Catastrophe Management
description: The ability to declare catastrophe events, activate surge protocols, deploy field resources, and manage high-volume claims processing with modified SLAs and approval authorities.

name: Claim Record
description: Master claim record containing claim metadata, status, amounts, parties, and lifecycle events.

name: Claimant
description: Person or entity making the claim. Includes contact information, identification, and claim history.

name: Policy Reference
description: Reference to the insurance policy associated with a claim. Includes coverage terms, limits, and deductibles.

name: Adjudication Decision
description: Record of the adjudication decision including coverage determination, payment amount, and rationale.

name: Reduce Claims Cycle Time
description: Reduce average claims cycle time from 14 days to 7 days by Q4 2026 through automation and process optimization.

name: Improve Adjudication Accuracy
description: Improve automated adjudication accuracy from 82% to 95% by enhancing rules engine and incorporating ML models.

name: ADR-0042: Real-time Adjudication
description: Decision to implement real-time adjudication for auto claims under $5,000 using a dedicated fast-path rules engine, bypassing the batch queue.

name: ADR-0043: Event-driven Claims
description: Decision to migrate claims processing from request-response to event-driven architecture using Kafka for improved decoupling and scalability.

name: REST API First
description: All new services must expose REST APIs following the enterprise API design guidelines. SOAP interfaces are prohibited for new development.

name: PII Data Handling Standard
description: Requirements for handling personally identifiable information including encryption, access controls, audit logging, and retention policies.

name: AWS Deployment Standard
description: All new workloads must be deployed to AWS using approved patterns: ECS Fargate for services, Lambda for event handlers, RDS/Aurora for databases.

name: Claims Adjuster
description: Insurance professional responsible for evaluating and processing claims.

name: Policyholder
description: Customer who holds an insurance policy and may file claims.

name: Policy Admin System
description: Core policy administration system managing policy lifecycle from quoting through renewal. Handles all lines of business.

name: Policy Rating Engine
description: Calculates premiums based on risk factors, coverage selections, and actuarial tables. Supports real-time and batch rating.

name: Policy Document Generator
description: Generates policy documents, endorsements, declarations pages, and certificates of insurance.

name: Policy API
description: REST API for policy CRUD operations, endorsements, and renewals.

name: Policy Database
description: Primary policy data store with full history and audit trail.

name: Policy Event Bus
description: Kafka-based event bus for policy domain events (issued, renewed, cancelled, endorsed).

name: Rating API
description: High-performance rating calculation API.

name: Rating Tables Store
description: Redis-cached actuarial rating tables and factors.

name: Policy Issuance
description: The ability to issue new insurance policies across all lines of business.

name: Premium Calculation
description: The ability to calculate premiums based on risk factors and coverage selections.

name: Policy Renewal
description: The ability to process policy renewals including re-rating and document generation.

name: Policy Rating API
description: REST API for real-time policy rating calculations. Handles quote rating, renewal re-rating, and endorsement re-rating across all lines of business.

name: Policy Rules Engine
description: Business rules engine for underwriting decisions. Evaluates risk acceptability, coverage eligibility, and pricing exceptions using Drools-based rule sets.

name: Policy Document Store
description: Document storage for policy PDFs, endorsements, and declarations pages. S3-backed with metadata indexing and versioning.

name: Policy Workflow Engine
description: Orchestrates policy lifecycle workflows including new business, renewal, endorsement, and cancellation. Camunda BPM-based with custom task handlers.

name: Policy Cache
description: Redis cache layer for frequently accessed policy data, rate tables, and territory factors. Reduces database load by 70%.

name: New Business Underwriting Process
description: End-to-end new business workflow: application intake → risk assessment → underwriting decision → quote generation → binding → policy issuance. SLA: 48 hours for standard auto, 5 days for specialty.

name: Policy Renewal Process
description: Automated renewal process: renewal eligibility check → re-rating → renewal offer generation → member notification → acceptance/decline → policy reissue. 90-day advance processing window.

name: Endorsement Processing
description: Mid-term policy change workflow: change request → impact assessment → re-rating → approval routing → endorsement issuance → billing adjustment.

name: Underwriting
description: Ability to assess risk, determine insurability, and price policies based on risk factors, loss history, and underwriting guidelines.

name: Policy Lifecycle Management
description: Ability to manage the full policy lifecycle from quote through renewal, including endorsements, cancellations, and reinstatements.

name: Policy Record
description: Core policy data object: policy number, effective dates, coverage limits, deductibles, premium, named insureds, covered vehicles/properties.

name: Rate Table
description: Rating factor tables: territory factors, vehicle symbols, driver classifications, multi-policy discounts, loyalty tiers. Updated quarterly by actuarial.

name: Billing System
description: Core billing system managing premium billing, payment processing, and accounts receivable for all lines of business.

name: Payment Gateway
description: Payment processing gateway supporting credit card, ACH, and wire transfers. PCI DSS Level 1 compliant.

name: Billing API
description: REST API for billing operations.

name: Billing Database
description: Billing data store with financial transaction history.

name: Payment Processor
description: Core payment processing service integrating with Stripe and bank APIs.

name: Payment Processor
description: External payment processing integration hub. Handles credit card, ACH, and EFT transactions via Stripe and internal banking APIs.
nfr_encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

name: Payment Ledger
description: Double-entry accounting ledger for premium receivables, payments, refunds, and write-offs. PostgreSQL with strict ACID compliance.

name: Payment Processor API
description: Payment gateway integration layer. Handles tokenization, authorization, capture, and refund workflows across Stripe and internal banking APIs.

name: Billing Notification Service
description: Sends billing reminders, payment confirmations, and past-due notices via email, SMS, and push notifications.

name: Billing Scheduler
description: Scheduled job runner for installment generation, auto-pay processing, and grace period enforcement. Runs nightly and on-demand.

name: Premium Billing Process
description: Monthly billing cycle: installment generation → payment collection → reconciliation → grace period management → cancellation for non-payment. Processes ~200K installments monthly.

name: Payment Processing
description: Real-time payment workflow: payment receipt → validation → authorization → capture → ledger posting → confirmation. Supports credit card, ACH, and EFT.

name: Premium Collection
description: Ability to collect premiums through multiple payment methods, manage installment plans, and handle payment failures gracefully.

name: Financial Reconciliation
description: Ability to reconcile premium receivables with actual payments, identify discrepancies, and manage refunds/write-offs.

name: Payment Transaction
description: Payment record: transaction ID, amount, method, authorization code, status, timestamps, associated policy number.

name: Monitoring Platform
description: Centralized monitoring and observability platform. Collects metrics, logs, and traces from all systems.

name: Incident Management System
description: Incident detection, routing, and resolution management. Integrates with PagerDuty and Slack.

name: Log Analytics Platform
description: Centralized log aggregation and analysis platform. ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) providing full-text search, log correlation, and anomaly detection across all systems.

name: Deployment Platform
description: CI/CD and container orchestration platform. Kubernetes (EKS) + Harness for automated deployments with blue-green and canary release strategies.

name: Monitoring Dashboard
description: Grafana dashboards for real-time system health, SLA tracking, and capacity metrics. 45+ dashboards covering all tier-1 and tier-2 systems.

name: Alert Manager
description: PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration for incident routing, escalation policies, and on-call scheduling. Routes alerts from Datadog to appropriate on-call teams.

name: Log Collector
description: Filebeat/Fluentd log collection agents aggregating logs from all containers. Enriches logs with service metadata before forwarding to OpenSearch.

name: Incident Tracker
description: ServiceNow integration for incident lifecycle management, RCA tracking, and SLA compliance reporting.

name: Deployment Pipeline
description: Harness deployment pipelines for blue-green and canary releases. Supports automated rollback on health check failure.

name: Kubernetes Cluster
description: EKS Kubernetes cluster hosting containerized workloads. 12 node groups across 3 AZs with cluster autoscaler and Karpenter for node provisioning.

name: Incident Management Process
description: Incident lifecycle: detection → triage → assignment → investigation → resolution → post-mortem → RCA documentation. SLA: P1 = 15 min response, 4 hour resolution.

name: Change Management Process
description: Change request workflow: RFC submission → impact assessment → CAB review → approval → implementation → validation → closure. All production changes require CAB approval.

name: Capacity Planning Process
description: Quarterly capacity review: utilization trend analysis → growth projection → infrastructure sizing → procurement request → provisioning. Covers compute, storage, and network.

name: Observability
description: Ability to monitor, trace, and debug distributed systems through metrics, logs, and traces with real-time alerting.

name: Release Management
description: Ability to safely deploy software changes to production with automated testing, staged rollouts, and instant rollback.

name: Incident Response
description: Ability to detect, respond to, and resolve production incidents within SLA targets with structured post-mortem processes.

name: Ensure Regulatory Compliance
description: Ensure all P&C systems and processes comply with state insurance regulations, NAIC guidelines, and internal audit requirements. Zero critical audit findings target.

name: Improve Member Experience
description: Deliver seamless digital experiences across quoting, servicing, and claims with <3 second page loads and >4.5 app store rating.

name: Operational Resilience
description: Achieve 99.95% availability across all tier-1 systems with <15 minute incident detection and <4 hour resolution SLAs.

name: Member
description: USAA member (policyholder/claimant). Interacts with Claims Portal, Policy Admin, and Billing System via web and mobile applications.

name: Underwriter
description: P&C underwriter. Uses Policy Admin System for risk assessment, exception handling, and manual review of complex applications.

name: External Vendor
description: Third-party service providers: body shops, medical providers, contractors. Interact via Claims Portal API for assignment acceptance and status updates.

name: API Design Standard
description: RESTful API design standard: OpenAPI 3.0 spec required, semantic versioning, OAuth 2.0 auth, rate limiting, pagination. Applies to all API containers.

name: Data Classification Standard
description: Data classification standard: PII handling (encryption at rest AES-256, in transit TLS 1.3), retention policies, access controls per NIST 800-53.

name: Cloud-Native Architecture Standard
description: Cloud-native architecture standard: 12-factor app principles, containerized deployment, horizontal scaling, health checks, graceful shutdown.

name: ADR: Event-Driven Architecture
description: ADR: Adopt event-driven architecture using Apache Kafka for inter-system communication. Rationale: decoupling, scalability, audit trail. Status: accepted.

name: ADR: API Gateway Pattern
description: ADR: Implement API gateway (Kong) for external-facing APIs. Rationale: centralized auth, rate limiting, observability. Status: accepted.

name: ADR: Database Per Service
description: ADR: Each system owns its database. No shared databases. Rationale: loose coupling, independent deployability, technology flexibility. Status: accepted.

name: RT Adjudication Service
description: New real-time adjudication microservice for fast-path claim processing. Handles auto claims under $5,000 with sub-second response time.

name: Billing Event Processor
description: New event-driven billing processor replacing batch billing runs. Processes billing events in real-time.
